events.text: Patient is a 63-year-old Hispanic male with a 50 pack-year smoking history (current smoker), ECOG performance status 1, BMI 24.6.

At age 63, patient was diagnosed with non-small cell lung cancer, squamous cell carcinoma (histology 8070), primary site right upper lobe (C34.1), clinical stage IIIA, T3N1M0, with chest wall involvement.

At age 63, patient presented with right-sided pleuritic chest pain and a chronic cough productive of blood-streaked sputum for 6 weeks. History of heavy tobacco use without prior lung cancer screening.

At age 63, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast showed a 5.4 x 4.7 cm mass in the right upper lobe abutting the chest wall with probable focal invasion of the parietal pleura. Right hilar lymph node enlarged to 2.2 cm. No distant metastases.

At age 63, PET/CT showed intensely FDG-avid right upper lobe mass (SUVmax 15.9) with chest wall contact and hypermetabolic right hilar lymph node (SUVmax 6.3). No distant metastatic disease. Incidental mild FDG uptake in bilateral hilar regions likely reactive.

At age 63, bronchoscopic biopsy with endobronchial ultrasound-guided transbronchial needle aspiration of right upper lobe lesion and station 11R lymph node. Right upper lobe: moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. Station 11R: positive for metastatic squamous cell carcinoma. Immunohistochemistry: p40 positive, CK5/6 positive, TTF-1 negative. PD-L1 TPS 45% (22C3 assay).

At age 63, next-generation sequencing (Guardant360 CDx) of circulating tumor DNA: EGFR wild-type, ALK rearrangement not detected, ROS1 rearrangement not detected, TP53 R175H detected, CDKN2A p16 loss detected, NFE2L2 mutation detected. No actionable driver mutations. Microsatellite stable.

At age 63, multidisciplinary tumor board discussion: stage IIIA (T3N1M0) squamous cell carcinoma with chest wall involvement. Potentially resectable with en bloc chest wall resection, but patient has limited pulmonary reserve (FEV1 1.8L, 55% predicted). Recommended neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy followed by surgical reassessment.
At age 63, initiated neoadjuvant carboplatin AUC 5, paclitaxel 175 mg/m2, and pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for 4 cycles.
At age 63, after cycle 1, patient reported grade 1 nausea, grade 1 fatigue, and grade 1 alopecia.
At age 63, after cycle 3, patient developed grade 2 nausea requiring additional antiemetic therapy with olanzapine. Grade 1 peripheral neuropathy noted.

At age 63, restaging CT after 4 cycles of neoadjuvant therapy showed major partial response. Right upper lobe mass decreased to 2.1 x 1.8 cm with retraction from the chest wall. Right hilar lymph node decreased to 0.9 cm.

At age 63, excellent response to neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy. Re-evaluation by thoracic surgery: mass now separated from chest wall, hilar lymph node near normal. Patient deemed surgical candidate.

At age 64, patient underwent right upper lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node dissection. Pathology showed major pathological response with only 5% viable tumor remaining. Margins negative. Lymph nodes 0/14 positive (complete nodal clearance). Pathologic stage ypT1aN0M0.
At age 64, initiated adjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for up to 1 year following surgery, per treatment protocol for resected NSCLC after neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy.
At age 64, after cycle 4 of adjuvant pembrolizumab, patient developed grade 1 hypothyroidism with TSH 8.7 mIU/L. Initiated levothyroxine 50 mcg daily.
At age 64, surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis 3 months postoperatively showed expected postsurgical changes in the right hemithorax. No evidence of recurrent or metastatic disease.
At age 64, surveillance CT 6 months postoperatively showed continued no evidence of disease. Postsurgical changes stable.
At age 65, surveillance CT 12 months postoperatively showed no evidence of recurrent or metastatic disease. Stable postsurgical changes in the right hemithorax.

At age 65, completed 1 year of adjuvant pembrolizumab (17 cycles). Tolerating well with managed hypothyroidism. No evidence of disease recurrence on serial imaging. Will continue surveillance with CT every 3 months for 2 years. Patient advised to remain tobacco-free and maintain follow-up.

At age 63, patient presented with right-sided pleuritic chest pain and a chronic cough productive of blood-streaked sputum for 6 weeks. History of heavy tobacco use without prior lung cancer screening.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Stage IIIA-IV non-small cell lung cancer without actionable driver mutations (EGFR wild-type, ALK negative, ROS1 negative). Treated with carboplatin/pemetrexed plus pembrolizumab immunotherapy. Mix of adenocarcinoma and squamous cell carcinoma. Some with high PD-L1 expression receiving pembrolizumab monotherapy.
